dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, my brother left {location} six weeks ago to deliver goods to a neighboring settlement. He never arrived. Nobody's seen him since.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The last anyone heard, he was camped {location_hint} when a storm hit. I found his campsite a week later: tent torn, supplies scattered, but no body and no blood. {target_npc} thinks the {enemy_type} might have taken him, but there's no proof either way. I've searched everywhere I can reach on my own, but the trail leads into dangerous territory and I'm no fighter. I need someone who can follow {quest_item} he left along his route: markers he always drops when he travels, little carved stones with our family crest.
dialogueChunks.outro: He's out there somewhere. I can feel it. I just need someone brave enough to find him.

playerResponses.text: Six weeks is a long time, but hope isn't lost. I'll follow the trail.
Tracking someone through hostile territory is dangerous work. What can you offer?
After six weeks, the odds of finding him alive are slim. I'm sorry.

dialogueChunks.intro: Please, {player_name}, you have to help me. My daughter wandered off during the {enemy_type} attack and nobody's seen her since.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: When the {enemy_type} hit {location} yesterday, everyone scattered. In the chaos, my daughter was separated from the group fleeing toward the shelters. {target_npc} saw her running toward the old mill {location_hint}, but nobody could follow because the {enemy_type} were between us and her. The attack has been repelled, but the roads aren't clear. She's seven years old, alone, and she doesn't know the forest. I need you to find {quest_item} she was carrying: her toy bear, her water skin, anything that shows which direction she went.
dialogueChunks.outro: She's smart and she's tough, but she's a child alone in the wild. Every hour counts.

playerResponses.text: A child alone out there? I'm already moving. Which direction was the mill?
I'll find her. Try to stay calm until I get back.
The forest is crawling with threats after that attack. I can't guarantee anything.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I've been looking for someone for a very long time. And I think I've finally found a lead.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} and I built {location} together, back when this was nothing but trees and mud. Three years ago, they left to establish a trade route through {location_hint} and promised to return within a season. That season turned into three years of silence. Last week, a trader passing through recognized {quest_item} I described: said they saw it in a camp near the edge of {enemy_type} territory. {target_npc} might be alive, might be captive, might be something I haven't considered. Either way, I owe it to them to find out.
dialogueChunks.outro: We swore we'd never abandon each other. I've already broken that promise once by waiting this long. Not again.

playerResponses.text: Three years of searching deserves a real answer. I'll find it.
Enemy territory with slim leads. Risky, but I understand the need. What's the pay?
Three years is a long time. You should prepare for the worst.
